questions.question: What percentage of actively managed large-cap funds underperform the S&P 500 over 15 years?
questions.explanation: According to the SPIVA scorecard, over 92% of actively managed large-cap funds underperform the S&P 500 over a 15-year period.

questions.question: Dollar Cost Averaging works by:
options: Investing only when prices are low
Investing a fixed amount at regular intervals
questions.explanation: DCA means investing a fixed dollar amount at regular intervals regardless of price. You automatically buy more shares when prices are low and fewer when prices are high.

questions.question: A value investor would most likely buy a stock with:
options: High P/E ratio and rapid revenue growth
Low P/E ratio and strong balance sheet
No earnings but huge market potential
The highest price in its sector
questions.explanation: Value investors seek stocks trading below their intrinsic value, typically characterized by low P/E ratios, strong balance sheets, and a margin of safety.

questions.question: The 'core-satellite' approach combines:
options: Index funds as a core with some active stock picks
questions.explanation: Core-satellite puts 80-90% in passive index funds (the core) and 10-20% in individual stocks or active strategies (satellites), combining reliability with potential upside.
